info.title: Canary API
info.description: Machine-consumable contract for the Canary observability API. Canary exposes deterministic, bounded responses for error ingestion, health checks, incidents, timelines, operator management, and webhook delivery visibility.

x-agent-guide.summary: Register webhooks as wake-up hints, then treat the timeline as the durable source of truth for correctness.
flow: Create a remediation claim before starting automated triage or repair work on an incident, error_group, target, or monitor.
Use POST /api/v1/events only for bounded operational analytics that help triage; do not encode analytics as errors.
Create a webhook subscription for the business events that should wake the agent.
Persist the last processed timeline cursor after each successful polling cycle.
When a webhook arrives, poll GET /api/v1/timeline from the last persisted cursor.
Process the returned events in order, then persist the response cursor before going idle again.
Write incident or error-group annotations back to Canary when the agent completes follow-up work.
x-agent-guide.webhook_contract: Verify x-canary-signature with the secret selected by x-webhook-id over `x-timestamp.x-delivery-id.body`, reject stale x-timestamp values in the receiver, and deduplicate deliveries by x-delivery-id. Canary also sends legacy x-signature over the body for older consumers. For disputed or failed deliveries, read `GET /api/v1/webhook-deliveries/{delivery_id}` for diagnostics, then replay durable state through `GET /api/v1/timeline` before acting. Webhooks are non-authoritative wake-up hints; the timeline is the durable event log and must be replayed for correctness.
x-agent-guide.crash_recovery: After a crash or restart, resume from the last persisted cursor, replay missed timeline events, and continue polling until the API returns no further cursor.
x-agent-guide.cursor_precedence: When both `after` and `cursor` are provided on timeline-style pagination endpoints, Canary resolves `after` first and ignores `cursor` for that request.
x-agent-guide.incident_entrypoint: On an `incident.opened` webhook, call `GET /api/v1/incidents/{id}` once — the response carries summary, the full incident metadata, a deterministic action brief, up to 25 correlated signals with per-type context, up to 20 newest annotations, and up to 5 most recent timeline events. Only fall through to /query, /timeline, or /errors/{id} when `signals_truncated` or `annotations_truncated` is true, or the agent needs raw stack traces.
x-agent-guide.scope_semantics: `x-canary-required-scope` names the least-privilege key scope to provision for the operation. Admin keys may also satisfy read-only and ingest-only route permissions at runtime, but agents should request the narrow scope unless they are managing Canary configuration.

cold_start.entrypoint: When an agent has no saved cursor, call GET /api/v1/report?window=1h first. The report is the bounded current-state snapshot for targets, monitors, active incidents, and active error groups.
cold_start.pagination: If report.truncated is true or report.cursor is non-null, keep calling /api/v1/report with that report cursor until the response is no longer truncated. Report cursors are only for report pagination and are not timeline cursors.
cold_start.handoff: After the bounded report snapshot is exhausted, switch to GET /api/v1/timeline for durable replay. Do not pass report.cursor to timeline; start timeline with an agent-chosen after timestamp for the report window boundary, or omit cursor/after to read the default timeline window, then persist the returned timeline cursor.

annotation_write_back.summary: Annotations are loose coordination notes on incident, error_group, target, or monitor subjects. Use remediation claims for active ownership; use annotations for evidence, PR links, and decisions.
annotation_write_back.subject_policy: Prefer the most specific durable subject: incident for incident-level decisions, error_group for code fixes, target/monitor for health remediation. Do not use annotations as commands to mutate repos from Canary.

summary_exceptions.reason: Public liveness/readiness and the OpenAPI document are self-describing infrastructure probes or the contract itself; agents do not need a triage summary for these responses.

Creation/update responses are deterministic receipts with ids, state, or one-time secret material; agents should persist the fields and then replay report or timeline when they need synthesized state.
Admin collection responses are configuration inventories. Their item arrays are already the complete machine-readable state and are not agent triage summaries.
Diagnostic row/page responses expose canonical ledger or check fields for reconciliation; agents inspect status, attempts, ids, and timestamps directly rather than trusting prose synthesis.
RFC 9457 Problem Details responses are structured error contracts with status, code, and detail; agents handle them through error branching rather than triage summaries.

claim_protocol.summary: Claims are typed ownership records for agentic remediation. Use annotations for notes and claims for who owns active work.
claim_protocol.conflict_policy: POST /api/v1/claims returns 409 Problem Details with current_claim when another active, unexpired claim owns the subject.
claim_protocol.idempotency: Replay the same idempotency_key for the same subject to recover the existing claim instead of creating a duplicate.

telemetry_model.summary: Canary owns errors, health checks, check-ins, incidents, annotations, remediation claims, and bounded analytics events that help agents triage operational state.

201.description: Check-in stored and monitor state updated.

x-agent-guidance.when_to_call: Use from non-HTTP runtimes to report a configured monitor heartbeat, progress marker, success, or error without creating an HTTP target.
x-agent-guidance.trust: The response is a committed write receipt: monitor_id, check_in_id, state, observed_at, and sequence are durable store values.
x-agent-guidance.next: For synthesized health state, read GET /api/v1/status or replay GET /api/v1/timeline; do not infer incidents from the receipt alone.

get.description: Provide `service`, `error_class`, or `group_by=error_class`. Service-scoped queries default to `window=1h`; error-class and group-by queries default to `window=24h`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for grouped error pagination.

200.description: Unified report in JSON or CSV form.

x-agent-guidance.when_to_call: Use for cold start or periodic full-state inspection when the agent lacks a saved cursor or needs a bounded current snapshot.
x-agent-guidance.trust: The deterministic summary, cursor, truncated flag, and returned targets/monitors/incidents/error_groups describe the current bounded window.
x-agent-guidance.next: Follow report.cursor until truncated is false, persist the checkpoint, then switch to timeline replay for durable incremental state.

get.description: If both `after` and `cursor` are supplied, `after` takes precedence.

x-agent-guidance.when_to_call: Use after cold start and whenever a webhook wakes the agent; pass the saved cursor/after value to replay durable events.
x-agent-guidance.trust: Timeline events are the durable source of truth for ordered state replay; webhooks are hints and may be duplicated or delayed.
x-agent-guidance.next: Process events in order, persist the returned cursor before going idle, and fetch detail endpoints only when an event is truncated or needs raw context.

get.summary: Page through webhook delivery ledger rows
get.description: If both `after` and `cursor` are supplied, `after` takes precedence.

get.summary: Inspect one webhook delivery by stable delivery id
get.description: Use this diagnostic endpoint after deduplicating by `x-delivery-id`; replay `/api/v1/timeline` before taking action because webhooks are wake-up hints, not durable state.

get.summary: List recent checks for a target
Get incident detail with bounded signals, annotations, and timeline
get.description: One-call payload for responder agents: deterministic summary, incident metadata, a read-only action brief derived from existing state, up to 25 correlated signals, up to 20 newest annotations, and up to 5 most recent timeline events. Truncation is signalled by `signals_truncated` and `annotations_truncated`.

x-agent-guidance.when_to_call: Use as the first detail call after an incident.opened or incident.updated event references an incident id.
x-agent-guidance.trust: The response summary, action_brief, signals, annotations, and recent timeline are deterministic and bounded; truncation booleans identify when to fetch more.
x-agent-guidance.next: If signals_truncated or annotations_truncated is true, fetch timeline, query, or annotations pages before acting; otherwise write back an annotation when follow-up completes.

post.summary: Create an annotation for an incident

x-agent-guidance.when_to_call: Use to record incident-level triage, fix proposal, verification, or dismissal once downstream work has produced evidence.
x-agent-guidance.trust: The response is the stored annotation row and the annotation.added webhook is a wake-up hint for other consumers.
x-agent-guidance.next: Persist any external references in metadata, then replay timeline or incident detail to confirm the coordination record is visible.

get.summary: List annotations for an error group

post.summary: Create an annotation for an error group

x-agent-guidance.when_to_call: Use to record code-fix or classification work against a stable error_group when the same issue may appear in multiple incidents.
x-agent-guidance.trust: The response is the stored annotation row; Canary does not interpret metadata beyond preserving and emitting it.
x-agent-guidance.next: Query the group or incident detail before duplicate remediation, and replay timeline after write-back if another agent may act on it.

get.summary: List annotations for any signal subject
get.description: Returns annotations attached to the specified subject, newest-first, with cursor pagination (max 50 per page). Supports subject_type: incident, error_group, target, monitor. Responders use this to read prior coordination signal on the underlying subject (e.g. has anyone already filed a PR for this error_group?).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response. Pass to fetch the next page.

post.summary: Create an annotation on any signal subject
post.description: Responders stamp opaque metadata (PR links, ack tokens, fix references) onto an incident, error_group, target, or monitor. Canary owns the surface; consumers own the content. Fires an annotation.added webhook with signed HMAC + X-Delivery-Id ledger entry.

x-agent-guidance.when_to_call: Use for generic write-back to incident, error_group, target, or monitor subjects when subject_type and subject_id are already known.
x-agent-guidance.trust: The response is the stored annotation row with opaque metadata preserved exactly as submitted.
x-agent-guidance.next: Use stable action values where possible, keep metadata opaque, and rely on consumers rather than Canary for remediation behavior.

post.summary: Connect a service and return onboarding snippets

get.summary: List remediation claims for a subject
get.description: Returns bounded durable remediation claim state for an incident, error_group, target, or monitor. Agents use current_claim to decide whether work is already owned.

post.description: Atomically claims a subject for agentic remediation. Active unexpired claims conflict with 409 Problem Details carrying current_claim. Replaying the same idempotency key for the same subject returns the existing claim.

200.description: Existing claim returned for idempotent replay.

409.description: Active claim conflict. The body includes current_claim.

get.description: Returns one claim row by CLM id within the caller tenant/project/service authority.

post.description: Moves a claim through the bounded remediation state machine and appends evidence links.
Marks a claim released so another agent can claim the subject later.
Stores a typed analytics event as a telemetry.event timeline row. Use this for low-volume operational/product signals that help agents triage; do not send logs, metrics, traces, or raw PII.

x-agent-guidance.when_to_call: Call when a consuming app or agent needs to record a low-volume typed event that helps correlate user flows, deploys, or agent work with errors, health, and incidents.
x-agent-guidance.trust: The caller owns redaction before ingest. Canary enforces size, shape, scoped API keys, and service authority, but retention_class and privacy_policy are policy labels until downstream retention/privacy automation consumes them.
x-agent-guidance.next: Read /api/v1/report for recent_events or replay /api/v1/timeline?event_type=telemetry.event with a cursor to verify the event and correlate it with incidents, errors, health, claims, and annotations.

bearerAuth.description: Send `Authorization: Bearer sk_live_...` with an `ingest-only`, `read-only`, or `admin` API key.

UnauthorizedProblem.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked API key.

RateLimitedProblem.description: The client exceeded a per-key or per-IP rate limit.

NotFoundProblem.description: The requested resource does not exist.

PayloadTooLargeProblem.description: The request body exceeded a size limit.

ForbiddenProblem.description: API key is valid but lacks the required scope.

last_error_class.description: Sanitized failure class such as runtime_error or panic; never a raw error message.

observed_at.description: Optional runtime observation time. Canary rejects values more than 5 minutes in the future to prevent check-ins from deferring overdue alerts.

annotation_count.description: Count of annotations on this signal's underlying error_group subject. Responders use this to skip re-investigation when prior coordination signal already exists.
Count of annotations on this signal's underlying target/monitor subject.

action.description: Deterministic recommendation derived from current incident state. It is guidance for responder agents, not an authoritative workflow state.

signals_truncated.description: True when signal_counts.active/resolved are based on the visible signal window rather than the full incident signal set.

incident_id.description: Populated for subject_type=incident. Legacy compatibility field.

group_hash.description: Populated for subject_type=error_group. Legacy compatibility field.

action.description: Opaque action label. Canary guarantees the x-canary-stable-values labels for cross-agent coordination but still stores other strings for consumer-owned workflows.

metadata.description: Opaque metadata preserved by Canary. Expected keys are optional coordination hints, not Canary behavior switches.

action.description: Opaque action label. Canary guarantees the x-canary-stable-values labels for cross-agent coordination but still stores other strings for consumer-owned workflows.

metadata.description: Opaque JSON object preserved by Canary. Expected keys are optional coordination hints, not Canary behavior switches.

summary.description: Deterministic summary of annotations on the subject (total count + latest agent).

cursor.description: Opaque cursor for fetching the next page, or null when the current page is the last.

action.description: Opaque action label. Canary guarantees the x-canary-stable-values labels for cross-agent coordination but still stores other strings for consumer-owned workflows.

metadata.description: Opaque JSON object preserved by Canary. Expected keys are optional coordination hints, not Canary behavior switches.

allow_private.description: Compatibility flag. Private target URLs are accepted only when the server is configured with ALLOW_PRIVATE_TARGETS=true.

service.description: Optional service binding. When set, Canary only sends events for matching service payloads.
Optional service binding. Service-scoped webhooks receive only matching service events.
Optional service binding. When set, Canary only sends events for matching service payloads.
Service scope copied from the event payload at enqueue time.
Optional service binding for non-admin ingest/read keys. Service-bound keys cannot access or ingest for other services.

allow_private.description: Compatibility flag. Private target URLs are accepted only when the server is configured with ALLOW_PRIVATE_TARGETS=true.

ApiKeyScope.description: Authorization scope attached to an API key.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor for fetching the next page, or null when all claims are visible.

name.description: Consumer event name such as checkout.completed.
